title: How George Saunders’s Analysis of Russian Masters Provides a Critical Language for Audio Narratives
description: In 2022, George Saunders published A Swim in a Pond in the Rain, a book that shared his technique and structure for critical reading and analysis of critical works by a quartet of Russian masters, offering a guide not only to would-be novelists and
hook: Saunders cracked the code for analyzing fiction—now writers are using his method to understand how podcasts actually work.

title: Traversing the Mahjong Multiverse
description: "As mahjong grows in popularity, can the diverging fanbases come together?"
hook: Mahjong fandom is fracturing into warring tribes. What does it mean when a game's community can't agree on what the game is?

title: The sublime, feral pleasure of Girl Dinner Diaries
description: In an age where it feels like we should all know less about each other, there’s at least one place on the internet where you just want to know more: It’s called r/GirlDinnerDiaries.  As its name suggests, the subreddit consists of women posting a photo of their meal accompanied by a brief story about what’s […]
hook: Women posting photos of their chaotic dinners with unexpectedly intimate stories—the internet's most honest food writing happening on Reddit.

title: Cannes Interview: Dominga Sotomayor on Her “Spontaneous and Liberating” La Perra
description: Dominga Sotomayor’s cinema is one of confined spaces. Her features tend to unfurl in tight, growingly claustrophobic settings. In her 2012 debut Thursd
hook: A filmmaker talks spontaneity on set, the violence of control, and how a dog taught her to stop overthinking.

title: the thing between the wall and my body
hook: What does your body actually experience in a prison cell? A physical essay on incarceration that reads like science fiction.
